brand.purpose: The 'Sandcastle' wordmark; solid ink fill beside the castle mark. Never gradient.

h1.purpose: The single empty-state headline; balanced wrap.

title.purpose: Panel headers, section and card headings.

label.purpose: Chip keys and small meta; not all-caps by default.

mono.purpose: Anything machine-generated: tool summaries, URLs, config values, generated source.

extensions.gradientPolicy: The No-Gradient Rule: Sandcastle uses no gradient anywhere — not the wordmark, not the Build button, not a bubble, not a background wash. Emphasis is weight, size, solid fill, or a hairline.

shadows.name: shadow-sm
shadows.purpose: Contact shadow for resting raised controls (Build button, topbar).

shadows.name: shadow-md
shadows.purpose: Soft lift for the two workspace panels off the paper.

shadows.name: shadow-lg
shadows.purpose: Reserved for the transient toast, the one truly floating layer.

materials.name: sheet
materials.value: background: var(--surface); border: 1px solid var(--line); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md);
materials.purpose: Flat opaque white sheets (panels, cards). No translucency, no blur — never glass.

materials.name: recessed
materials.purpose: Recessed fills: mono tool rows, code view, skeletons.

materials.name: lane-wash
materials.purpose: Faint per-agent tint behind a badge or bubble; tracks the lane's own hue.

motion.name: state-standard
motion.purpose: Border-color, background, and color state transitions.

motion.name: row-in
motion.purpose: New transcript row entrance (staggered by arrival, not uniform).

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Required fallback; infinite loaders/blink/pulse/shimmer settle to a stable state.

breakpoints.name: stack
breakpoints.purpose: Below this, the two-pane workspace stacks vertically (log over proof).

breakpoints.name: compact
breakpoints.purpose: Phone: file explorer stacks, brand tag hides, tighter padding.

components.name: Primary Button (Build)
components.description: The one loud control: solid indigo fill, near-white text, never wraps. Exactly one on screen at a time.
components.css: .ds-build { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; padding: 9px 16px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #254dac; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 13.5px; white-space: nowrap; color: #fdfcff; background: #3461c9; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(31,39,52,0.07); transition: background 0.15s ease; } .ds-build:hover { background: #254dac; } .ds-build:disabled { opacity: 0.45; cursor: not-allowed; box-shadow: none; }

components.name: Ghost / Icon Button
components.description: Secondary actions (Share, reload, external): opaque surface with a hairline stroke.
components.css: .ds-ghost { padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #dadee3; background: #fdfdfe; color: #1f2734; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; transition: background 0.15s ease, border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-ghost:hover { background: #edf1f4; }

components.name: Config Chip
components.description: Run-config metadata pill: a label-weight key in ink-faint and a MONO value in ink. Status only, never a control.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 4px 9px; border: 1px solid #dadee3; border-radius: 8px; background: #fdfdfe; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; } .ds-chip-key { color: #636975; font-weight: 600; font-size: 11px; } .ds-chip-val { color: #1f2734; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12.5px; }

components.name: Agent Badge
components.description: Icon-led tag in the active agent's hue as legible ink on a faint wash (Planner blue / Builder amber / Fixer green). The load-bearing signal of the build transcript.
components.css: .ds-agent { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 3px 9px; border-radius: 999px; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; border: 1px solid transparent; } .ds-agent-ico { width: 14px; height: 14px; stroke: currentColor; fill: none; } .ds-agent-planner { color: #0065b4; background: color-mix(in oklab, #0065b4 9%, #fdfdfe); border-color: color-mix(in oklab, #0065b4 30%, #dadee3); }

components.name: Prompt Composer
components.description: The build prompt field: opaque surface, hairline stroke, auto-grow, shared accent focus ring. Placeholder is AA ink-faint, not a washed gray.
components.html: <textarea class="ds-input" placeholder="Describe an app to build…"></textarea>
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; resize: none; padding: 12px 14px; border-radius: 11px; border: 1px solid #dadee3; background: #fdfdfe; color: #1f2734; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.5; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #636975; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3461c9; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Transcript Tool Row
components.description: A monospaced tool-call line hung off the timeline spine with a lane marker; grounding (Learn/MCP) rows tint violet.
components.css: .ds-trow { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 22px 1fr; gap: 10px; align-items: start; } .ds-marker { color: #007842; } .ds-tool { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12.5px; color: #1f2734; background: #edf1f4; border-radius: 5px; padding: 2px 6px; overflow-wrap: anywhere; } .ds-trow-ground .ds-tool { color: #7444b4; }

narrative.overview: Sandcastle is a bright, structural engineering instrument — a build log you can read across a room. Built for a skeptical developer on a phone or a spectator at a booth screen in a bright hall, it is light, high-contrast, and legible on a projector. A two-pane workspace: the left pane is the build transcript (a real timeline with agent lanes and mono tool rows), the right pane is the proof (live preview + real source). Color is information, not decoration: each agent owns a hue rendered as legible ink on light. There is no gradient and one committed indigo accent for the single Build action. Depth is honest — flat opaque surfaces, hairline borders, a restrained real shadow scale. It is a deliberate rejection of the dark neon-glass 'AI devtool dashboard' reflex.
keyCharacteristics: Bright drafting-paper two-pane workspace; the frame recedes, the live log leads.
Semantic per-agent color (blue / amber / green) as legible ink, always paired with icon + label.
No gradient anywhere; one committed indigo accent reserved for the single Build action.
Flat opaque surfaces with hairline borders and a restrained real shadow scale — never glass.
Real inline-SVG icons and a custom castle mark — zero emoji. IBM Plex Sans + IBM Plex Mono, self-hosted.

rules.name: The No-Gradient Rule
rules.body: Sandcastle uses no gradient anywhere — not the wordmark, not the Build button, not a bubble, not a background wash. Emphasis is weight, size, solid fill, or a hairline.

rules.name: The Color-Is-Meaning Rule
rules.body: Blue = Planner/interactive, Amber = Builder, Green = Fixer/success, Violet = grounding, Red = error, Indigo = the one primary action. Color signals which agent or state you're looking at, and every colored element also carries an icon or label so meaning survives grayscale and color-blindness.

rules.name: The Mono-Means-Real Rule
rules.body: Anything machine-generated (tool summaries, URLs, config values, generated source) is set in IBM Plex Mono; human prose is set in IBM Plex Sans. The font is a truth signal.

rules.name: The Flat-Opaque Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat and opaque. Convey depth with tone, a hairline border, and a restrained real shadow — never translucency, blur, or glass. To make a surface pop, raise its shadow one step or strengthen its border.

dos: Do keep color semantic and pair every colored element with an icon or label.
Do set machine output in IBM Plex Mono and human prose in IBM Plex Sans.
Do build depth from flat opaque surfaces, hairline borders, and the restrained real shadow scale.
Do keep exactly one primary (Build) action on screen at a time.
Do use real inline-SVG icons (1.6px stroke, currentColor) and the castle mark — never an emoji.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
Do keep the empty state varied (how-it-works timeline + featured starter + mixed chips), never an identical card grid.
donts: Don't reach for the dark neon-glass 'AI devtool dashboard' reflex — no backdrop-filter glass, no radial neon spotlights, no five-accent glow.
Don't use a gradient anywhere — not the wordmark, the Build button, a bubble, or a background.
Don't use emoji as iconography; every glyph is a real SVG.
Don't collapse the agent process into a single chat bubble or plain LLM text box.
Don't turn it into a sterile SaaS dashboard (KPI-card grids, hero-metric template).
Don't fake or hide the artifact; the Code tab and live preview prove it's real.
Don't add colored border-left/right stripes; use full hairline borders and lane washes.
Don't introduce a warm cream/sand body background; the desk is cool near-white, warmth lives only in Builder amber.
Don't add display/serif fonts or spend a signal hue on decoration.
